site.name: Bitfield
site.description: Public docs for Bitfield, the plug-in runtime and database for products that need to stay fast as they grow.

pages.title: Bitfield Docs
pages.description: Start here if you want to understand Bitfield and use the public Runtime Kit surface.
pages.summary: The public entry point for Bitfield docs.

pages.title: Quickstart
pages.description: Get a key, add Runtime Kit, read data, and send a request.
pages.summary: The shortest path from a Bitfield key to the first app-facing Runtime Kit calls.

pages.title: Get your key
pages.description: How keys, checkout, account access, and the first device activation fit together.
pages.summary: Explains what happens when you get a Bitfield key and activate your first device.

pages.title: What is Bitfield?
pages.description: A customer-facing explanation of Bitfield as a database and runtime.
pages.summary: Explains Bitfield without assuming the reader already knows database or runtime vocabulary.

pages.title: Database and runtime
pages.description: What Bitfield stores, what it runs, and why those two pieces belong together.
pages.summary: Defines the public database and runtime model.

pages.title: Storage shape
pages.description: How Bitfield keeps local durable data fast without turning private internals into public docs.
pages.summary: Explains local durable truth, memory-mapped local files, content identity, and the current view.

pages.title: Content-addressed storage
pages.description: What content addresses are and why they matter for speed, integrity, and reuse.
pages.summary: Defines content addresses in plain words and states what the public docs do not expose.

pages.title: Slots
pages.description: What a Bitfield slot is and why slots keep features from tangling together.
pages.summary: Explains slots as named runnable doors that features can call without importing each other.

pages.title: Active devices
pages.description: What counts as an active device and how revoking or replacing a device works.
pages.summary: Defines active devices for pricing, keys, and account management.

pages.title: JavaScript Runtime Kit
pages.description: Use the app-facing JavaScript and React surface.
pages.summary: Shows the public JavaScript Runtime Kit shape without exposing Bitfield setup work.

pages.title: Read data in React
pages.description: Use the public React read hook without taking on Bitfield setup details.
pages.summary: Shows how useBitfieldData returns data, loading, and error for app components.

pages.title: Send a request
pages.description: Send opaque payloads to Bitfield targets through the public request function.
pages.summary: Shows how sendRequestToBitfieldTarget sends a target plus payload and returns reply bytes.

pages.title: Packages
pages.description: How Runtime Kit packages declare what should be stored and run.
pages.summary: Documents the public package boundary file and package-set idea.

pages.title: Local state
pages.description: What lives under the Bitfield local state folder and what each area means.
pages.summary: Documents customer-visible local state at a high level.

pages.title: Build your own surface
pages.description: How to design another app-facing SDK without exposing Bitfield setup details.
pages.summary: Defines the public SDK boundary pattern: simple app functions outside, Bitfield setup behind the plug.

pages.title: Trials and billing
pages.description: How the free trial, monthly and yearly cadence, active-device ranges, and legal links fit together.
pages.summary: Explains trial billing and active-device ranges without turning pricing into hidden math.

pages.title: How Bitfield is this fast
pages.description: A speed proof with category boundaries.
pages.summary: Explains warm local reads, memory mapping, content addresses, and non-claims.

pages.title: Warm and cold paths
pages.description: What each benchmark category includes and what it leaves out.
pages.summary: Defines warm local read, cold local read, content address lookup, durable batch write, runtime call, and network command.

pages.title: Benchmark ledger
pages.description: Public benchmark claims with category boundaries and non-claims.
pages.summary: Lists public speed claims with the work category attached.

pages.title: Measurement methodology
pages.description: How to read Bitfield benchmark numbers without comparing unlike jobs.
pages.summary: Explains warm reads, cold reads, hash lookup, durable writes, and why each number needs its category.

pages.title: Runtime Kit API
pages.description: The current public Runtime Kit values.
pages.summary: Reference for useBitfieldData and sendRequestToBitfieldTarget.

pages.title: Claim ledger
pages.description: The fields every public benchmark claim must carry.
pages.summary: Documents the public claim-ledger fields that keep speed claims honest.

pages.title: Package boundary
pages.description: Reference for things-to-store-and-run.json.
pages.summary: Lists the public package boundary fields and thing types.

pages.title: Changelog
pages.description: Customer-facing docs and product updates.
pages.summary: The public change log for docs and customer-visible product behavior.
